title: Fails to SAT solve, but why?

features.name: multiple always_ff blocks driving same signal
features.description: Code should include two separate always @(posedge clk) blocks that both drive the same signal q1
features.snippet:
always @(posedge clk) begin q0 <= q0_next; end
always @(posedge clk) begin q1 <= q0_next; end

features.name: same register drives multiple outputs
features.description: Code should include a register q0_next driven by always_comb that is used to assign two different output signals q0 and q1 in separate always @(posedge clk) blocks
features.snippet:
reg [7:0] q0_next;
always_comb begin if (we0 && raddr0 == waddr0) q0_next = din0; else q0_next = rf[raddr0]; end
always @(posedge clk) begin q0 <= q0_next; end
always @(posedge clk) begin q1 <= q0_next; end
